notes: Sets CR expiration date of January 30, 2026

provisions.description: Entitlements and mandatory payments whose budget authority was provided in the referenced appropriations Acts, and Food and Nutrition Act activities, shall continue at the rate to maintain program levels under current law through January 30, 2026. Obligations for mandatory payments due on or about the first day of any month after October 2025 but within 30 days of the CR expiration may continue.

provisions.description: Requires compensation of furloughed State employees and reimbursement of States and Federal grantees for expenses that would have been paid by the Federal Government during a lapse in appropriations, including interest. Applies to any lapse period in FY2026.

notes: Subsection (a) covers compensation and reimbursement rules

provisions.description: Prohibits use of federal funds for reductions in force (RIF) during the CR period. Applies to all civilian positions. Exceptions for voluntary separations, court orders, and orderly shutdown during a lapse. Retroactively nullifies any RIF actions taken between October 1, 2025 and enactment, requiring reinstatement and backpay for affected employees.
provisions.raw_text: (a) Notwithstanding section 101, section 1605 of title VI of division A of Public Law 119–4 shall be applied through the end of the last applicable pa
notes: Significant policy rider prohibiting federal workforce reductions
Subsection (a) establishes the prohibition during the CR period
Subsection (b) applies to all civilian positions regardless of type or funding source
Subsection (c) provides exceptions for voluntary separations, court orders, and orderly shutdown
Subsection (d) defines reduction in force broadly including 5 USC 3501-3504, 3595, and similar reductions
Subsection (e) retroactively nullifies any RIF actions taken between Oct 1, 2025 and enactment
Subsection (e)(1) requires reinstatement and backpay per section 116
Subsection (e)(2) requires agency notice to affected employees and Appropriations Committees within 5 days
Subsection (e)(3) requires notices to include reinstatement date and backpay amount

provisions.description: Amends Section 10 of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005 (2 U.S.C. 6628) to establish comprehensive requirements for notification when Senate data is sought through legal process, including notification duties for providers and the Office of the SAA, special rules for target and non-target investigations, a private cause of action with statutory damages of $500,000 per instance, waiver of sovereign immunity, and limited retroactive applicability to actions occurring on or after January 1, 2022.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: Section 10 of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005 (2 U.S.C. 6628) is amended—
notes: Defines 'covered data', 'Senate data', 'target of a criminal investigation'
Requires providers and Office of the SAA to notify Senate offices upon receipt of legal process
Bars courts from issuing gag orders preventing notification except for target investigations (max 60-day delay, renewable)
Creates private cause of action with $500,000 statutory damages per instance and no immunity defense
Waives sovereign immunity
Retroactive to January 1, 2022
5-year statute of limitations from actual notice of violation

cross_references.target: Section 10 of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005 (2 U.S.C. 6628)
cross_references.description: Senate data notification and protection requirements

notes: Subsection (a). The core farm bill extension provision. Extends all authorities of the 2018 Farm Bill (and amendments thereto) at mandatory funding levels as in effect on September 30, 2025 (including pursuant to the American Relief Act, 2025). provisions.description: Requires the Comptroller General to submit a report to Congress assessing the supply chain of over-the-counter monograph drugs, including overall stability, FDA information collection and use, interagency coordination, and unique characteristics of the OTC monograph drug marketplace.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Not later than September 30, 2027, the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions
notes: GAO report required under SEC. 6505(b)

Report must assess: overall supply chain stability, FDA information collection, FDA use of supply chain information for regulatory decisions, interagency coordination to monitor/mitigate disruptions, and additional authorities or information FDA may need

provisions.description: Amends Section 505G of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to add new subsection (r) requiring the Secretary to utilize real world evidence standards for evaluating the safety and effectiveness of active ingredients for topical administration, apply subsection (b)(6)(C) to topical drugs, consider non-animal testing methods, and issue draft guidance on nonclinical testing alternatives within 1 year. Also requires that any final administrative order on nonprescription sunscreen active ingredients account for historical safety data, the role of broad spectrum sunscreens in cancer prevention, and incorporate the new evidence and testing standards.
provisions.policy_area: FDA regulation of nonprescription topical drugs and sunscreen
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 6506. 
Treatment of active ingredients for topical administration
(a) 
In general
Section 505G of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355h) is amended by adding at the end the following:
notes: Subsection (a) adds new subsection 505G(r) with three paragraphs: (1) evidence standards allowing real world evidence, (2) non-animal testing methods, and (3) a clarification that nothing alters GRASE or section 505 standards
Subsection (b) imposes requirements on the sunscreen final administrative order under Section 3854 of the CARES Act (P.L. 116-136)

cross_references.target: Section 505G of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355h)
cross_references.description: Adds new subsection (r) on evidence and testing standards for topical active ingredients

provisions.description: Amends Section 505(b) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to add new paragraph (7) establishing a framework for Rx-to-nonprescription switches, including meeting procedures for applicants, requiring FDA to issue guidance within 18 months on the process and standards for approval of Rx-to-OTC switch applications, and requiring a stakeholder engagement plan within 1 year. Defines full and partial Rx-to-nonprescription switches.
provisions.policy_area: FDA regulation of Rx-to-nonprescription drug switches
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 6507. 
Increasing the clarity and predictability of the process for developing applications for Rx-to-nonprescription switches

(a) 
In general
Section 505(b) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355(b)) is amended by adding at the end the following:
notes: Subsection (a) adds new paragraph 505(b)(7) with subparagraphs (A) through (E) covering meetings, guidance, stakeholder engagement, definitions, and rule of construction
Guidance must be issued within 18 months of enactment; stakeholder engagement plan within 1 year
Defines both 'full' and 'partial' Rx-to-nonprescription switches

provisions.description: Requires the Comptroller General of the United States to submit a report within 1 year of enactment evaluating Rx-to-nonprescription switch applications approved since October 1, 2022, including the number approved, those with additional conditions, average review times, partial switches, barriers to timely review, FDA stakeholder engagement, and opportunities for collaboration between CDER and CMS on analyzing health insurance claims data.
deadlines: 1 year after the date of enactment

(b)

In general

SEC. 6509. 
Sunset dates

(a) 
Authorization
Sections 744L and 744M of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 379j–71; 379j–72) shall cease to be effective October 1, 2030.
notes: Subsection (a) sets authorization sunset for Sections 744L (definitions) and 744M (user fee authorization) at October 1, 2030
Subsection (b) sets reporting requirements sunset for Section 744N at January 31, 2031

provisions.description: Provides that amendments made by Title V take effect on October 1, 2025, or the date of enactment of this Act, whichever is later, except that OTC Monograph Drug User Fees under part 10 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the FFDCA shall be assessed beginning October 1, 2025, regardless of the date of enactment.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 6510. 
Effective date
The amendments made by this title shall take effect on October 1, 2025, or the date of the enactment of this Act, whichever is later, except that fees under part 10 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 379j–71 et seq.) shall be assessed beginning October 1, 2025
notes: Ensures fee assessment begins October 1, 2025 even if the bill is enacted after that date
All other Title V amendments take effect on the later of October 1, 2025 or enactment

cross_references.description: OTC Monograph Drug User Fee provisions

provisions.description: Provides that notwithstanding the amendments made by Title V, the prior version of part 10 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the FFDCA (OTC Monograph Drug User Fee provisions) as in effect on the day before enactment shall continue to be in effect for purposes of assessing and collecting fees for fiscal years prior to fiscal year 2026.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 6511. 
Savings clause
Notwithstanding the amendments made by this title, part 10 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 379j–71 et seq.), as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of this Act, shall continue to be in effect with respect to assessing and collecting any fee required by such part for a fiscal year prior to fiscal year 2026.
notes: Standard savings clause preserving the prior fee authority for pre-FY2026 fee obligations
Ensures continuity of fee collection for any outstanding obligations under the prior authorization

provisions.description: Makes multiple substantive improvements to the VA Partial Claim Program under section 3737 of title 38: (a) clarifies relationship to other powers of the Secretary under section 3720(h); (b) modifies administration including changing the subordinate lien calculation basis and extending the repayment timeline from 120 to 180 days; (c) clarifies that partial claims do not alter the guaranty calculation, are included in liquidation sales, and cannot be claimed under the guaranty; (d) modifies loan holder servicing requirements; (e) revises default and foreclosure provisions including borrower liability, administrative costs, and non-judicial sale discharge rules; (f) allows the Secretary to issue administrative guidance in advance of regulations for the Partial Claim Program and loss mitigation options.
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 7307. 
Improvements to Partial Claim Program of the Department of Veterans Affairs

(a) 
Clarification of relationship to other powers of Secretary
Sect
notes: Subsection (a) limits Secretary's purchase of guaranteed loans to cases consistent with section 3732(d)
Subsection (b)(1) changes subordinate lien basis from 'first lien guaranteed loan' to 'amount of indebtedness the Secretary does not purchase'
Subsection (b)(2)(A) extends borrower repayment period from 120 to 180 days
Subsection (b)(2)(B) rewrites partial claim payment treatment rules
Subsection (c) adds servicing requirement to loan holder obligations
Subsection (d) rewrites default liability and non-judicial foreclosure provisions
Subsection (e) broadens guidance-in-advance-of-regulations authority to include loss mitigation options under section 3732(d)
References notwithstanding provision in subsection (h) allowing guidance before regulations

Also requires an assessment of the benefits and challenges of the program not later than one year before it terminates.

provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 7308. 
Government Accountability Office reports on Partial Claim Program of the Department of Veterans Affairs and other matters

(a) 
Annual reports
notes: Subsection (a) requires annual GAO reports disaggregated by quarter covering: partial claims filed/approved, redefault/foreclosure rates, comparison to other loss mitigation options, housing loans insured/guaranteed/made, applications denied, refinances, and delinquency data (60+ and 90+ days late)

Subsection (b) requires a pre-termination assessment considering borrower characteristics, loan performance, cost comparison to other loss mitigation, comparison to COVID-19 Veterans Assistance Partial Claim Payment program, and comparison to other federal housing agencies' partial claim programs

provisions.description: Prohibits reprogramming, transfer of funds, or reimbursements under the Economy Act (or USDA organic act authorities) that create new programs, eliminate programs/projects/activities, increase funds or personnel for denied/restricted projects, relocate offices/employees, reorganize offices/programs/activities, or contract out functions performed by federal employees, unless the Secretary notifies and receives approval from the Appropriations Committees at least 30 days in advance. Also limits reprogramming involving funds in excess of $500,000 or 10 percent (whichever is less) that augments existing programs, reduces funding or personnel by 10 percent, or results from general savings from reduced personnel. Requires notification before implementing programs not carried out in the previous fiscal year. Prohibits modifying major capital investments exceeding $500,000 or 10 percent, realigning positions to establish entities with 5+ personnel, or carrying out activities not in the budget request without 30 days advance notice.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 716. (a) None of the funds provided by this Act, or provided by previous appropriations Acts to the agencies funded by this Act that remain avail
notes: Subsection (a) covers six categories of prohibited reprogramming without advance approval
Subsection (b) sets $500,000 or 10 percent threshold (whichever is less) for additional reprogramming restrictions
Subsection (c) requires approval before implementing new programs not carried out in previous FY
Subsection (d) covers capital investments, reorganizations of positions, and activities not in budget request
Subsection (e) requires written or email confirmation of receipt of notification
References 7 U.S.C. 2257 (section 702(b) of USDA Organic Act of 1944) and 7 U.S.C. 2263 (section 8 of P.L. 89-106)

SEC. 5002(a) is a sweeping extension of all Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 authorities (the 2018 Farm Bill) through at least September 30, 2026, with significant carve-outs in subsection (e) for specific mandatory funding streams.

SEC. 5002(g) makes the entire section retroactive to September 30, 2025, to prevent any gap in farm bill authority.
